conclusion: schedule_descriptor_get is a hard API limitation, not a code gap. Axxon schedules are authored in the desktop client and have no creatable or readable representation in the gRPC/HTTP API.

evidence.pdf: Integration APIs 3.0 (529 pages): 0 pages mention 'schedule', 'calendar', or 'weekly'.
evidence.proto_rpcs: No Create/Set/Add schedule RPC in any proto under axxonsoft/bl/. The only calendar surface is ArchiveSupport.GetCalendar, which returns days-with-recordings (read-only archive calendar), not a schedule object.
evidence.proto_messages: No schedule/calendar/weekday/timetable/recurrence message or field in any proto.
evidence.logic_macro: LogicService and Macro.proto have no schedule trigger or schedule condition.
evidence.live_config_graph: DomainService.ListComponents (501 components) contains 0 schedule/calendar objects; distinct trailing types are ACFA, EMULATOR_*, EMailModule, EventSupplier, MMExportAgent, MultimediaStorage, SinkEndpoint, SourceEndpoint, StateControl, TelemetryControl, src.
evidence.live_config_units: ConfigurationService.ListUnits full tree exposes no schedule/calendar unit type or key.

disposition: Documented limitation. No tool work is possible; closing this requires the desktop client, which is outside the API surface this MCP wraps.
